title: Pillowy Polish Pork Dumplings
description: These paleo-friendly dumplings are filled with a savory mixture of ground pork, ground clove, and lacquer-glaze, making for a delicious and energizing Polish dish.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground pork, ground clove, lacquer-glaze, salt, and black pepper, and mix well with your hands until just combined.
In a small bowl, whisk together the coconut flour and egg, then add the mixture to the pork mixture and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for about 5 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place a tablespoon of chicken broth in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and gently add the dumplings to the pot.
Cook the dumplings for 10-12 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Remove the dumplings from the water with a slotted spoon and serve hot.
